CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Set For Trial On Nov-06-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LAUREN B. FAZIO, Esq. of Collins + Collins LLP (Tel: 510-844-5100) for cross-defendants Anthony Clark and William Rapp;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS ANTHONY CLARK, AND WILLIAM RAPP'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Argument presented by attorney Ms. Fazio for cross-defendants. Matter submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-defendants Anthony Clark and William Rapp's "demurrer to Olivier Garnier dba Stucco d'Olivetti's first amended cross complaint" (FACC) is overruled.

The FACC pleads that Garnier bought workers' compensation insurance through Contractors Brokerage Service, a now-defunct corporation of which cross-defendants were principals. (Id. at 2:3-9, 21-24.) The FACC further pleads that Contractors Brokerage "held itself out as a specialist for contractors," and had a "legal duty to adequately explain the insurance policies it was selling to Garnier." (Id. at 5:4-8, 18-23.) No explanation occurred and the state's assignee has now sued Garnier for $100,000+ for misclassifying workers as independent contractors. (Id. at 3:23-4:3.) The demurrer asserts duty and statute-of-limitations grounds.

Duty: As shown above, the FACC adequately pleads duty. Cross-defendants argue that Contractor's Brokerage had no "legal duty to adequately explain the insurance policies it was selling," but cite no authority for the notion. (Memo. 6:1-8.) Moreover, it is conceded that an insurance broker may assume additional duties by holding itself out as a specialist, as the FACC pleads Contractors Brokerage did here. (Id. at 5:11-14.)
Limitations. The time-bar argument relies on an FACC paragraph mentioning a state audit begun in December 2018. However, Garnier points out that the state did not sue until 2021. (Opp. 6:5-10.)

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

(Atty. VLADIE P. VILTMAN, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran, LLP (Tel: 415-673-5600) appears late via Zoom videoconference for defendant/cross-complainant Olivier Garnier dba Stucco D'Olivetti. Mr. Viltman is informed of the outcome of the hearing.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS ANTHONY CLARK, WILLIAM RAPP's Demurrer to First Amended Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. The meet and confer declaration does not comply with CCP 430.41(a)(3)(A) because it does not explain the "means" by which the parties met and conferred. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the amended cross-complaint is now due by September 9, 2022.
